worth_it_explanation: Amazon DSP runs across a wide range of placements, from display on third-party sites to streaming TV (STV) on Prime Video and Fire TV. Each placement type has format requirements, and a line item that lacks the right format simply will not compete in auctions for that placement. For endemic advertisers, responsive e-commerce creatives (RECs) pull live product data from an Amazon Standard Identification Number (ASIN) and adapt to placement sizes automatically. A campaign without RECs leaves the most Amazon-native format off the table. These checks are a configuration review of the creative library and the line item assignments. They take minutes to run and often surface formats that are missing or assigned to the wrong line items.
intro: Amazon DSP supports several distinct creative categories: standard display (desktop and mobile browser), online video, streaming TV, audio, and responsive e-commerce creatives. Each category is matched to different placement types in the ad server. A line item buying STV inventory needs a video asset that meets STV specifications. A line item buying Amazon-owned retail placements can benefit from a REC that pulls live ASIN data. These checks confirm that the creative library covers the formats the line items actually need, that RECs are in use where retail goals are present, and that no high-volume format is absent.

audit_checks.title: Creative library lacks a format required by the placements a line item is buying
audit_checks.impact_rationale: A line item that bids on a placement type without a compatible creative assigned to it will not win impressions for that placement. The budget allocated to the line item either sits unspent or concentrates on the subset of placements that do have a matching asset, which can skew delivery and inflate frequency on the covered placements.

educate.base:
Amazon DSP serves ads across several distinct placement environments: desktop and mobile browser display, mobile app banners, online video, streaming TV (STV) on Prime Video, IMDb TV, and Fire TV, and audio on Amazon Music. Each environment requires a different creative format. A standard display banner cannot serve in an STV slot. A video asset built for online video pre-roll does not meet STV specifications, which require a non-skippable 15-second or 30-second video at specific resolution and bitrate minimums.

The creative library in Amazon DSP organizes assets by format type. When a line item is configured to buy STV inventory, Amazon DSP looks for a creative that meets STV requirements before it can bid. If no qualifying creative is attached to that line item, the line item is effectively excluded from STV auctions regardless of the targeting, bid, and budget set on it.

The same principle applies to audio, online video, and display. A campaign running across several placement categories but uploading only one creative type will only deliver on the placements that format covers.

A few signs point to format coverage gaps:
  • A line item is targeting STV supply but no video creative meeting STV specifications is attached to it
  • The creative library contains only display banners but the order includes line items buying streaming or audio placements
  • Delivery reports show one placement type absorbing most impressions while others show near-zero delivery despite being in the targeting
You may want to open each line item and confirm the attached creative types against the supply sources the line item is targeting before drawing conclusions from delivery data.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: For an endemic advertiser with retail goals, format coverage also affects access to Amazon-owned retail placements such as detail page ads and search results placements on Amazon.com. Those placements often favor responsive e-commerce creatives (RECs), so a creative library that contains only standard display banners may limit delivery on the highest-intent Amazon retail surfaces. You may want to confirm that RECs are present in addition to standard display.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: For a non-endemic advertiser driving to an off-Amazon destination, the priority is confirming that the formats assigned to each line item match the placements in the media plan. A campaign designed to include STV for brand awareness alongside display for retargeting needs qualifying creative for each. You may want to review the line item's targeting settings alongside the creative types attached to confirm the match.

learn_more.source_title: How to launch and optimize your streaming TV campaign

investigate.intro: Open each active line item in the Amazon DSP console and compare the supply sources in its targeting against the creative types attached to it.

amazon_dsp_console.label: Amazon DSP console
steps: Open the order, then select each active line item.
In the line item settings, locate the supply source targeting and note which placement types are included, for example display, online video, STV, or audio.
Open the Creatives tab for the same line item and note the format type of each creative attached.

For each supply type in the targeting, confirm that at least one attached creative meets the format requirements for that type. STV requires a non-skippable video meeting STV specifications. Audio requires an audio asset. Display requires a standard banner at an accepted size.
Flag any line item where the targeting includes a placement type that no attached creative covers.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records, per active line item, the supply types in its targeting and the creative format types attached. A line item passes when every supply type it targets has at least one attached creative that qualifies for that type. A line item fails when at least one supply type in its targeting has no qualifying creative, recorded with the line item name and the missing format type.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record the supply types targeted and the creative formats attached for each line item above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that one or more active line items on {{site_name}}'s Amazon DSP account are targeting placement types for which no qualifying creative is attached. A line item cannot bid on a placement type it has no compatible creative for, so the budget allocated to those placements is either unspent or displaced onto other placements, concentrating delivery and inflating frequency where creatives do exist. {{voice_recommend}} auditing the creative assignments on each affected line item, uploading assets that meet the specifications for the targeted placement type, and confirming in delivery reporting that impressions distribute across placement types as the media plan intends.

audit_checks.title: Responsive e-commerce creatives are absent from line items with retail goals
audit_checks.impact_rationale: Responsive e-commerce creatives (RECs) are the format Amazon DSP built for endemic retail campaigns. They pull live ASIN data, such as product title, star rating, Prime badge, and price, from the product detail page and adapt to the placement size automatically. A retail-goals line item using only standard display banners shows a static asset where a format with live product signals could improve click-through and conversion. The gap is not catastrophic but it is a meaningful forfeit of Amazon-native capability.

educate.base:
A responsive e-commerce creative (REC) is an Amazon DSP creative type that assembles itself from a product detail page rather than from a static image file. The advertiser supplies an Amazon Standard Identification Number (ASIN), and the REC pulls the product title, main image, star rating, review count, Prime eligibility badge, and current price from that ASIN's detail page at serve time. Amazon DSP then adapts the layout to fit the placement size, so the same creative renders as a 300x250 display unit on a desktop browser, a mobile banner in an app, or a native format on an Amazon-owned surface.

Because the creative reads from the detail page, it reflects current data. If the product goes out of stock, if the price changes, or if review count grows, the creative updates without the advertiser uploading a new asset. This also means a REC attached to an out-of-stock ASIN will display a product that cannot convert, which is a separate risk to confirm.

RECs are only available for endemic advertisers whose products are sold on Amazon. Non-endemic advertisers driving to off-Amazon destinations cannot use them. For endemic advertisers with purchase or retail-outcomes goals, the format is the most native to the buying experience Amazon offers through DSP.

A few signs suggest RECs are missing where they could help:
  • Line items with a retail outcomes or conversions goal are attached only to standard display banners
  • The creative library contains no RECs despite the account running endemic campaigns
  • The account is buying Amazon Owned and Operated Network supply, where RECs are the preferred format, but the line items carry only static banners
You may want to confirm which ASINs are actively in stock and eligible before creating RECs, then assign them to the line items targeting Amazon-owned retail placements.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: For an endemic advertiser, RECs are particularly relevant on Amazon Owned and Operated Network supply, which includes Amazon.com display placements, Amazon app placements, and IMDb. Those surfaces are high-intent shopping environments. You may want to confirm that active ASINs with in-stock products have RECs created and assigned to line items targeting Amazon-owned inventory.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: RECs require a product sold on Amazon and are not applicable to non-endemic advertisers driving to off-Amazon lead forms or brand sites. This check does not apply for non-endemic campaigns.

learn_more.source_title: Maximize your campaign performance with the Amazon Owned and Operated Network

investigate.intro: Confirm whether RECs are present in the creative library and whether they are assigned to line items with retail or conversion goals.

amazon_dsp_console.label: Amazon DSP console
steps: Open the creative library and filter by creative type. Look for any creatives listed as 'Responsive e-Commerce Creative' or 'REC'.
If RECs exist, note the ASINs attached to each one. For each ASIN, open the Amazon product detail page to confirm the product is in stock and the listing is active.
Open each active line item with a retail outcomes or conversions goal. In the Creatives tab, note whether RECs are attached alongside or in place of standard display banners.
For line items buying Amazon Owned and Operated Network supply, note whether at least one REC is assigned.
Flag any line item with a retail or purchase goal that carries only standard display banners and no REC.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records whether the creative library contains RECs, whether each REC is attached to an active and in-stock ASIN, and whether line items with retail or purchase goals have at least one REC assigned. An endemic campaign with at least one REC assigned to each retail-goals line item passes. An endemic campaign with retail-goals line items carrying only standard display banners and no RECs is a fail recorded with the line item names. Non-endemic campaigns are excluded from this check.
evaluate.fallback_message: Confirm whether RECs are present in the creative library and whether they are assigned to retail-goals line items above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that {{site_name}}'s Amazon DSP campaigns with retail or purchase goals are not using responsive e-commerce creatives (RECs). RECs pull live product data from the ASIN detail page, including price, star rating, and Prime badge, and adapt to placement sizes automatically. Line items with retail goals running only static display banners are missing the format that Amazon DSP designed for endemic retail campaigns, particularly on Amazon-owned inventory where REC formats are the most native to the shopping experience. {{voice_recommend}} creating RECs for active, in-stock ASINs and assigning them to line items targeting Amazon Owned and Operated Network supply with retail outcomes or purchase goals.

audit_checks.title: Streaming TV line items lack a compliant video creative
audit_checks.impact_rationale: Streaming TV (STV) on Amazon DSP buys non-skippable video impressions on Prime Video, IMDb TV, and Fire TV. Those are premium, lean-back placements where the viewer cannot skip the ad. A line item targeting STV that lacks a qualifying video creative cannot bid on those placements, losing access to them entirely. When the issue goes unnoticed, delivery falls short of the media plan without a clear reason visible in reporting.

educate.base:
Streaming TV in Amazon DSP delivers non-skippable video ads to viewers on Prime Video, IMDb TV, Freevee, and Fire TV apps. These placements reach audiences in a lean-back, high-attention environment that differs from display or online video pre-roll. Amazon DSP enforces format requirements for STV that are stricter than for general online video.

STV creatives must be non-skippable video, typically 15 or 30 seconds in length. Amazon requires a minimum video resolution of 1920x1080 pixels, a specific bitrate range, and audio levels within broadcast standards. Assets that meet online video specifications but fall short of STV requirements are rejected at the creative upload stage or flagged during review, leaving the line item without a qualifying creative to bid with.

A line item configured to buy STV supply without a qualifying creative attached to it will not enter STV auctions. If the line item also targets display or online video, all delivery shifts to those placements, which may not reflect the media plan. The STV portion of the budget goes unspent or is reallocated by pacing logic to other placements.

A few signs point to an STV creative problem:
  • A line item includes STV in its supply targeting but delivery reports show no STV impressions after the first 48 hours of flight
  • The creative library contains video assets that were uploaded but remain in a 'rejected' or 'under review' status for STV
  • The only video assets in the account were produced for online video pre-roll and have not been re-encoded to meet STV specifications
You may want to check the creative status on any line item that targets STV and compare the video asset's technical specifications against the Amazon Ads STV requirements before assuming the placement is simply competitive.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: For an endemic advertiser, STV is a brand-awareness format. It builds awareness at scale ahead of display retargeting that drives to the product detail page. You may want to confirm that the video asset tells a coherent brand or product story in 15 or 30 seconds without relying on a clickable call to action. STV placements are non-interactive on most connected TV surfaces.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: For a non-endemic advertiser, STV serves the same brand-building role. The asset needs to communicate the offer and direct viewers to a destination through verbal or on-screen messaging alone. You may want to confirm the asset includes a clear verbal call to action since connected TV viewers cannot click on the ad.

learn_more.source_title: How to launch and optimize your streaming TV campaign

investigate.intro: Open each line item that targets STV supply and confirm the video creative attached to it meets Amazon STV specifications and has passed creative review.

amazon_dsp_console.label: Amazon DSP console
steps: Open the order and select a line item that includes streaming TV in its supply targeting.
Open the Creatives tab for that line item and review the status of each attached video creative. Look for any creatives showing a rejected or under-review status.

For each video creative attached to an STV line item, open the creative detail and note the video duration, resolution, and file format. Confirm it meets the non-skippable STV requirements (15 or 30 seconds, 1920x1080 minimum resolution).
If no video creative is attached, or if all attached creatives are standard display banners rather than video, flag the line item.

Check delivery reporting for the line item. Filter by placement type or supply source and confirm whether STV impressions appear. Near-zero STV delivery with STV in the supply targeting and budget available is a signal that no qualifying creative is reaching auction.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records, per line item targeting STV supply, the status and technical specifications of each attached video creative. A line item passes when at least one attached video creative has an active approved status and meets STV duration and resolution requirements. A line item fails when no attached creative qualifies for STV, recorded with the line item name and the creative status or missing specification.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record the STV line item names, attached creative statuses, and video specifications above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that one or more streaming TV line items on {{site_name}}'s Amazon DSP account lack a qualifying video creative, which prevents those line items from bidding on Prime Video, IMDb TV, and Fire TV placements. When no compliant STV creative is attached, the line item either goes dark on STV inventory or shifts all delivery to other placement types, leaving the streaming TV portion of the media plan unfulfilled. {{voice_recommend}} confirming that each STV line item has at least one attached video creative with an active approved status, a duration of 15 or 30 seconds, and a resolution of at least 1920x1080, and re-encoding any assets that were rejected or do not meet STV specifications.
